message:
feat(core): major refactor — crash recovery, journaled writes, compute budget, write-path VFS ops

Why these changes exist:

The core filesystem engine (ffs-core) was previously a read-only layer:
it could open ext4/btrfs images, resolve paths, read inodes, and serve
FUSE reads, but had no ability to modify the filesystem or recover from
unclean shutdowns.  This ~9300-line refactor transforms it into a
read-write engine with crash safety, resource awareness, and the VFS
mutation operations needed by the FUSE layer.

Crash recovery at mount time:
- On open, the engine now inspects the ext4 superblock state flags
  (EXT4_VALID_FS / EXT4_ERROR_FS / EXT4_ORPHAN_FS) to detect unclean
  shutdowns.  When the filesystem is dirty, it automatically replays the
  JBD2 journal, resets MVCC version chains to a known-good epoch, and
  records the outcome in a new CrashRecoveryOutcome struct that callers
  can inspect for diagnostics.
- After successful recovery the superblock is stamped clean
  (EXT4_VALID_FS) to prevent redundant replay on subsequent mounts.

Journaled write path:
- Introduces commit_transaction_journaled() which takes a set of block
  writes from an MVCC transaction, wraps them in a JBD2-compatible
  journal transaction (using the new Jbd2Writer from ffs-journal), commits
  descriptor/data/revoke/commit blocks to the journal region, then applies
  the writes to their home locations.  This gives FrankenFS the same
  crash-consistency guarantee as the Linux ext4 driver's ordered-data mode.
- The Ext4AllocState struct tracks per-group block/inode bitmaps and free
  counts at runtime, loaded at open time when write access is requested,
  feeding the persistent allocator from ffs-alloc.

Compute budget and degradation:
- Adds ComputeBudget, a monitor that periodically samples /proc/loadavg
  and converts the 1-minute load average into a headroom value (0.0–1.0)
  relative to available CPU cores.  This feeds into the asupersync
  SystemPressure handle so that all layers can query current load.
- Defines the DegradationPolicy trait for policies that adjust internal
  parameters (cache sizes, flush intervals, compression thresholds) in
  response to system pressure changes.

Write-path VFS operations:
- Implements the FsOps trait methods for setattr, write, create, mkdir,
  unlink, rmdir, rename, symlink, link, and truncate — the full set of
  mutation operations that the FUSE layer dispatches.
- Each write operation acquires an MVCC transaction, performs the logical
  mutation (inode update, directory entry insertion/removal, block
  allocation/deallocation), commits through the journal path, and returns
  updated attributes to the caller.
- SetAttrRequest struct provides a structured representation of the
  partial-update semantics required by FUSE setattr (each field optional).

Dependency additions (Cargo.toml):
- Adds ffs-journal (for Jbd2Writer), crossbeam-epoch (for EBR-based
  version reclamation in MVCC), parking_lot Mutex (for alloc state), and
  libc (for errno constants in write error paths).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
